name: Canakinumab reduced recurrent cardiovascular events without lipid lowering
summary: CANTOS is the strongest direct human proof that targeted cytokine inhibition can reduce a major age-related disease outcome.
statement: In 10,061 post-myocardial-infarction patients with hsCRP of at least 2 mg/L, canakinumab 150 mg every 3 months lowered recurrent major cardiovascular events without lowering lipid levels.
population_or_model: Patients with previous myocardial infarction and residual inflammatory risk
quantitative_note: The 150 mg dose had a primary-endpoint hazard ratio of 0.85 versus placebo (95% CI 0.74 to 0.98; P=0.021) and met the prespecified multiplicity-adjusted threshold; all-cause mortality was not significantly different.
caveats: The benefit was in secondary cardiovascular prevention, not healthy aging or frailty prevention.
Canakinumab was associated with a higher incidence of fatal infection than placebo.
Only the 150 mg dose met the prespecified multiplicity-adjusted threshold for the primary endpoint.
